title: Dombeya sparmannioides
intro: Dombeya sparmannioides is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dombeya sparmannioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dombeya sparmannioides is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be used in the form of a tincture or powder to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Dombeya sparmannioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Africa.
